channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Google’s AI summaries are changing search. Now it’s facing a lawsuit
item.description: For more than two decades, users have turned to search engines like Google, typed in a query, and received a familiar list of 10 blue links—the gateway to the wider web. Ranking high on that list, through search engine optimization (SEO), has become a $200 billion business.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: The Rings of Power season 3 adds Stranger Things' Jamie Campbell Bower and Heartstopper's Eddie Marsan to its cast, and I think they're perfect for two specific roles
item.description: The Rings of Power season 3's cats just got a big cast boost with the addition of two talented actors.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft is finally putting Skype out of its misery, urging people to use Teams instead
item.description: Remember Skype? Microsoft does, and it is finally deciding to pull the plug on one of the first big video chat applications for PC.
item.dc:creator: alekshamcloughlin@outlook.com (Aleksha McLoughlin), TechRadar

item.title: HP says it will move large parts of production out of China
item.description: After acquiring more stock last quarter, HP is planning to remove almost all of its US manufacturing away from China.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Stripe hits $91.5 billion valuation in latest tender offer
item.description: Stripe on Thursday announced a tender offer for employees and shareholders that valued the company at $91.5 billion, nearly 41% higher than its valuation a year ago, potentially delaying the fintech firm’s ambitions of going public.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Meme coins aren’t just harmless fun
item.description: For some time, meme coins have occupied a peculiar space in online culture. While there are people who have struck it rich trading these joke-based cryptocurrencies, the landscape is riddled with scams, “rug pulls,” and market manipulation.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: IBM closes Hashicorp buy, looks to boost enterprise multicloud and AI automation technology
item.description: In announcing the closure of its $6.4 billion buy of Hashicorp, IBM said its goal is to infuse HashiCorp automation and security technology in every data center possible.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New Google Chrome Split-Screen Feature Leaked in Beta Build - Exclusive Update for Web Browser Lovers
item.description: A new split-screen feature has been discovered in a Google Chrome Canary beta build, offering users the ability to use two windows within a single tab. The functionality is not yet live for regular users but shows promise for enhanced productivity and customization options.
item.dc:creator: alekshamcloughlin@outlook.com (Aleksha McLoughlin), TechRadar

item.title: Gabbard decries Britain's reported demand for Apple to provide backdoor access to users' cloud data
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard says she has serious concerns about the British government's reported demand that Apple provide backdoor access to any data stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Just what is an ‘IT worker’ now? The definition is changing
item.description: Not so long ago, the definition of an IT worker was pretty simple: they were largely confined to central roles related to hardware, network infrastructure, and software development in centralized IT departments.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Vietnam Revises Energy Plans to Focus on Solar Power
item.description: Vietnam aims to get 16% of its energy from solar power, more than triple its earlier target of just 5%, and scraps plans for offshore wind turbines.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: What’s next for Microsoft’s Semantic Kernel
item.description: At the heart of Microsoft’s AI application development strategy is Semantic Kernel, an open source set of tools for managing and orchestrating AI prompts.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia revenue forecasts for first quarter exceed estimates
item.description: Nvidia forecast first-quarter revenue above market estimates on Wednesday, expecting robust demand for its leading AI chips to persist as businesses spend heavily to expand generative artificial intelligence infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia sales surge in the fourth quarter on demand for AI chips
item.description: The stock’s movement carries more weight on the S&P 500 and other indexes than any other company except Apple.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: iPhone bug that could suggest replacing 'racist' with 'Trump' is getting fixed
item.description: Apple is fixing a bug within the dictation feature on some iPhones that briefly suggests the word “Trump” when a word with an R consonant is spoken, including "racist.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: NFL NextGen Stats team unveils new tool to help fans better understand combine results
item.description: The NFL scouting combine provides a blizzard of data with hundreds of prospects running through various drills ranging from the 40-yard dash to the vertical jump to a short shuttle.
item.dc:creator: Josh Dubow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nvidia sales surge in the fourth quarter on demand for AI chips
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia on Wednesday reported a surge in fourth-quarter profit and sales as demand for its specialized Blackwell chips, which power artificial intelligence systems, continued to grow, sending the company's stock higher after hours. For the three months that ended Jan.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man who said he was upset with Musk and Trump charged with threatening to burn down xAI facility
item.description: ASHLAND CITY, Tenn. (AP) — A Tennessee man has been charged with an act of terrorism after he threatened to burn down an xAI facility because he was upset with its founder, Elon Musk, and President Donald Trump, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Critical infrastructure: The rising ransomware victim
item.description: Ferhat Dikbiyik, the chief research and intelligence officer at Black Kite, explains how critical infrastructure providers can better protect their systems. The post Critical infrastructure: The rising ransomware victim first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Las Vegas PD announce nation’s first fleet of police Tesla Cybertrucks
item.description: The Las Vegas Metropolitan Police Department (LVMPD) announced it had been gifted the country’s first fleet of Tesla Cybertrucks for law enforcement use. During an address, LVMPD said an unnamed donor gifted the department at least 10 Cybertrucks to use for patrol and SWAT teams.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: 8 ways to make Windows 11 work like Windows 10
item.description: Switching to Windows 11? You’re not alone. With the clock ticking down to Windows 10’s end-of-support date in October, professionals around the globe are making plans — and lots of them are making the upgrade to Windows 11.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: FCC chairman climbs WKRG broadcast tower to highlight, promote industry
item.description: The nation's top communications regulator made a stop in Baldwin County Monday morning and you might be surprised why.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Williams, The Hill

item.title: As Microsoft innovates, Apple stagnates
item.description: Microsoft and Apple were once the yin and yang of the tech world. Microsoft was dull and plodding, milking buggy, barely-good-enough-for-prime-time Windows for tremendous profits, thanks to a worldwide operating system monopoly.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cot framework aims to ease Rust web development
item.description: Web developers looking for an easier time with the Rust language may want to check out Cot, an emerging web framework pitched as being for “lazy developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Atlassian refocuses Trello on individual task management
item.description: Atlassian’s Trello project management tool has received a facelift with features designed to organize meetings and ideas in a cohesive manner.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New European AI weather model to improve forecasts
item.description: The European Center for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) has made its new AI model, the Artificial Intelligence Forecasting System (AIFS), available to everyone free of charge. Medium-range weather forecasts are those that predict weather conditions for three to 15 days into the future.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Elizabeth Holmes fails to overturn her Theranos fraud conviction
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Elizabeth Holmes, the disgraced founder of Theranos, will remain in prison after losing a bid Monday to overturn her fraud conviction, with a federal appeals court saying she hadn’t proved there were legal missteps during her trial for defrauding investors with false claims of w
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Apple announces $500 billion investment in US amid tariff threats that could affect the iPhone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Apple announced Monday that it plans to invest more than $500 billion in the United States over the next four years, including plans to hire 20,000 people and build a new server factory in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Sweden is investigating a damaged cable in the Baltic Sea
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Swedish authorities said on Friday they were investigating a damaged cable that was discovered in the Baltic Sea, the latest in a string of recent incidents of ruptured undersea cables that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4
